2606.10747.title: The Arbiter Agent: Continually Monitoring Multi-Agent Conversations to Detect Emergent Misalignment
2606.10747.paper_summary: As AI systems built from multiple language-model agents become more common, they are increasingly used to make decisions together: discussing, negotiating, and acting on shared tasks. While individual agents may appear well-aligned when tested on their own, problems can arise from how they interact with one another. We introduce the Arbiter, an agent designed to monitor multi-agent conversations in real time and identify which participants may be behaving in misaligned ways. The Arbiter operates under a limited "inspection budget", meaning it must decide carefully how to use its resources. As it observes a conversation step by step, it can choose to wait, question a participant, examine internal information such as system prompts or reasoning traces, or log concerning behavior. At the end, it produces a report identifying the likely source of misalignment. We evaluate the Arbiter across five conversation conditions, ranging from risky financial advice model organisms to evaluation-aware and colluding agents, we test five tool configurations of increasing capability and two backbone models. We find that the Arbiter reliably detects misaligned agents well before the end of the conversation, with active inspection tools improving both detection accuracy and speed. Weight-induced misalignment proves hardest to detect, while instruction-induced misalignment is identified reliably even under passive observation. The logging tool exhibits a dual effect, improving recall at the cost of precision. These results suggest that continual, budget-aware monitoring can effectively catch misalignment, and that overseeing multi-agent systems may require treating the auditor as an active participant in the process. The code is available at https://github.com/aisilab/arbiter.
2606.10747.paper_summary_zh: 隨著由多個語言模型代理組成的人工智慧系統變得越來越普遍，它們被用來共同做出決策：討論、談判和執行共享任務。雖然單獨測試時，各個代理可能看起來相當一致，但它們之間的互動可能會產生問題。我們介紹了仲裁者（Arbiter），這是一個旨在實時監控多代理對話並識別哪些參與者可能行為不一致的代理。仲裁者在有限的「檢查預算」下運作，這意味著它必須仔細決定如何使用其資源。在逐步觀察對話的過程中，它可以選擇等待、詢問參與者、檢查內部信息（例如系統提示或推理痕跡），或記錄令人關注的行為。最後，它會生成一份報告，識別出可能的偏差來源。我們在五種對話條件下評估仲裁者，這些條件從風險金融建議模型生物到評估意識和共謀代理不等，我們測試了五種能力逐漸增強的工具配置和兩個主幹模型。我們發現，仲裁者能夠在對話結束之前可靠地檢測出不一致的代理，並且主動檢查工具提高了檢測的準確性和速度。由權重引起的偏差最難檢測，而由指令引起的偏差即使在被動觀察下也能可靠識別。記錄工具顯示出雙重效果，提高了召回率但以精確度為代價。這些結果表明，持續的、考慮預算的監控能有效捕捉偏差，並且監督多代理系統可能需要將審計者視為過程中的主動參與者。代碼可在 https://github.com/aisilab/arbiter 獲得。
2606.10747.author: Filippo Tonini et.al.
2606.10747.authors: Filippo Tonini, Federico Torrielli, Anton Danholt Lautrup, Peter Schneider-Kamp, Mustafa Mert Çelikok, Lukas Galke Poech
